cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Anthony could have gone to the bookstore. First, let me list out the information given: 1. Anthony woke up at 7am. 2. David saw Anthony working at the office from 7am to 8am. 3. Leslie saw Anthony getting coffee at the cafe from 8am to 11am. 4. Sarah saw Anthony watching a movie at the theater from 1pm to 9pm. 5. The bookstore was closed after 9pm. So we need to find the time slots when Anthony wasn't doing any of those activities and also before the bookstore closed at 9pm. Let's break down Anthony's schedule based on the given information: - 7am to 8am: Working at the office (David saw him). - 8am to 11am: Getting coffee at the cafe (Leslie saw him). - 1pm to 9pm: Watching a movie (Sarah saw him). - The bookstore was closed after 9pm, so he must have gone before that. So let's identify the gaps where Anthony was not doing any of the above activities. Anthony woke up at 7am, so before that time, we don't have any information, but since he woke up at 7am, we can assume he wasn't doing anything significant before that. However, we need to consider the time when the bookstore could be open. Bookstore is probably open in the morning, but we don't have a specific opening time. From 7am to 8am: Working at the office. From 8am to 11am: At the cafe. There's no information about Anthony's activities from 11am to 1pm. From 1pm to 9pm: At the theater. After 9pm: Bookstore is closed. So the only gap we can identify is from 11am to 1pm. That's when Anthony wasn't seen doing any activity. That would be the only time window when Anthony could have gone to the bookstore. Let me check the options: (A) 11am to 1pm (B) 8am to 11am (C) 7am to 8am (D) 1pm to 9pm Option (A) corresponds to the gap we identified (11am to 1pm). Option (B) is when Anthony was at the cafe, so he couldn't have been at the bookstore then. Option (C) is when Anthony was working at the office,
